verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal dual lipidation (myristoylation at Gly2 and palmitoylation at Cys3). These lipid modifications anchor LYN to the cytoplasmic face of the PM; no transmembrane domain exists and no extracellular-facing domain is present. Checking all contextual buckets: (1) Cancer ecto-display — some Src-family kinases have been reported on cancer-cell surfaces, but LYN specifically lacks robust documented outer-leaflet ecto-pool evidence; (2) Cell-death-induced display — no documented ICD/apoptosis outer-leaflet translocation for LYN protein body; (3) Germline/developmental surface restriction — LYN is broadly expressed in hematopoietic lineages but inner-leaflet anchored, not outer-leaflet; (4) Lysosomal exocytosis — LYN is not a lysosomal TM protein; (5) Stable TM-partner tethering — LYN associates with TM receptors (BCR, FcεRI) but on the cytoplasmic face, not via wash-resistant outer-leaflet tethering. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets LYN protein body on outer leaflet. Inner-leaflet anchored.
